channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: De productos baratos a símbolo de innovación: China gana terreno en Indonesia
item.description: Para muchos consumidores, “hecho en China” ya no significa bajo costo, sino tecnología y modernidad.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Hasya Nindita

media:content.media:credit: Ulet Ifansasti para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El mercado de Glodok, en el barrio chino de Yakarta, la capital de Indonesia, adornado por el Año Nuevo Lunar en febrero.

item.title: Algunos Labubus contienen algodón que podría ser producto de trabajos forzados
item.description: Una prueba verificada por The New York Times descubrió que la ropa de algunos de los muñecos virales contenía algodón procedente de la región china de Sinkiang, que está prohibido en EE. UU.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson, Sapna Maheshwari and Meaghan Tobin
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Jared Soares para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Para empresas como Pop Mart, vender productos que contengan algodón prohibido en Estados Unidos podría tener graves consecuencias.

item.title: Cómo los médicos de EE. UU. se aprovechan de una ley de protección al consumidor
item.description: El mecanismo diseñado para eliminar las facturas sorpresa ha abierto la puerta a reclamaciones millonarias y a una nueva dinámica en el sistema de salud.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kliff and Margot Sanger-Katz

media:content.media:credit: Andres Kudacki para The New York Times

item.title: ¿Qué pasa cuando la IA está a cargo de una tienda?
item.description: En San Francisco, Andon Market se anuncia como la primera boutique minorista gestionada por un agente de inteligencia artificial. Hasta ahora, el inventario parece arbitrario y hay demasiadas velas.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight

media:content.media:credit: Minh Connors para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Andon Market se anuncia como la primera tienda minorista del mundo que es gestionada por un agente de inteligencia artificial.

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Roasts Trump at a Mock White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: The late night host took “a page from the Kid Rock alternative halftime show,” which aired during the Super Bowl, in offering his own “all-American” version of the Washington tradition.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Los sueños de Trump de un acorazado llevaron a la destitución de su secretario de Marina
item.description: El secretario de Marina, John Phelan, debía entregar el primero de los barcos del presidente en 2028. El plazo era casi imposible.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Helene Cooper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: John Phelan, secretario de la Marina, en diciembre. La frustración del presidente Trump por la gestión de Phelan de su preciado programa de acorazados aumentó en las dos últimas semanas.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April, 24, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘solemn’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 214 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Cómo poner en orden tus finanzas en tiempos de crisis
item.description: Las noticias sobre la guerra y la incertidumbre laboral te pueden ofuscar, pero hay formas de mantener las riendas de tus egresos.
item.dc:creator: Connie Chang and Juli Fraga
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Rose Wong

item.title: Word of the Day: solemn
item.description: This word has appeared in 166 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Rubio Says Iran’s Soccer Team Can Attend World Cup
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that while the players were welcome, anyone accompanying them who has links to the Iranian military would be denied entry.
item.dc:creator: Qasim Nauman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at the White House on Thursday.

item.title: De los bombardeos a las tensas negociaciones: el estado de la guerra en Irán
item.description: El conflicto se ha transformado en un enfrentamiento volátil en el estrecho de Ormuz, a medida que aumentan los costes económicos y el presidente Trump se enfrenta a una reacción política interna.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Un mural en Teherán muestra el control de Irán sobre el estrecho de Ormuz.

item.title: I’m One of Cuba’s Political Prisoners. When Will I Go Free?
item.description: Nearly five years in Cuban prison have taught me that the government won’t loosen its hold on power.
item.dc:creator: Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara

item.title: DeepSeek’s Sequel Set to Extend China’s Reach in Open-Source A.I.
item.description: Chinese companies have embraced making their most advanced artificial intelligence models available to all.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin and Cade Metz

media:content.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Chinese start-up DeepSeek shook the industry in January 2025 with its claim that it had created an advanced A.I. system for a fraction of what its American rivals spent.

item.title: ‘Didi vs. Modi’: A Test for the Hindu Right in India’s Bengali Heartland
item.description: The party of Prime Minister Narendra Modi has been making gains in a state once beyond its grasp, but many decry an audit that removed nine million voters, many of them Muslim.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das, Hari Kumar and Atul Loke

media:content.media:description: Campaign posters featuring Prime Minister Narendra Modi of India, and Mamata Banerjee, the chief minister of West Bengal, who is also known by her moniker “Didi,” in Kolkata this month.

item.title: In Britain, 7 Unelected Lords Are Helping to Block an Assisted Dying Bill
item.description: A small group in the House of Lords has proposed hundreds of amendments that are helping stymie a bill that was meant to legalize medically assisted death for the terminally ill.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Aaron Chown
media:content.media:description: Members of the House of Lords and House of Commons during the State Opening of Parliament, in the House of Lords, in London, in 2023.

item.title: Europe Mulls What Mutual Defense Looks Like Outside NATO
item.description: European Union nations have a little-known obligation to protect one another. Experts caution it is no replacement for NATO.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger and Jeanna Smialek

media:content.media:credit: John Thys/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A meeting of NATO defense ministers at the group’s headquarters in Brussels in February. Europe is reconsidering its options for collective defense, as President Trump continues to threaten to leave the NATO alliance.

item.title: Corrections: April 24, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, April 24, 2026.

item.title: Instead of the Bouquet, She Caught a Groomsman’s Attention
item.description: Krystal Miller and Brandon Manning’s first meeting at a wedding was a bit awkward. But by their second date, their chemistry was undeniable, and Ms. Miller knew she was falling in love.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Menza

item.title: From Kissing One Guy in 20 Years to 11 in a Month
item.description: My husband leaving nearly broke me. I tried to put myself back together by dating for the first time in my life.
item.dc:creator: Erin Chack

media:content.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: War and Sanctions Accelerate China’s Currency Push
item.description: China’s bid to build a renminbi-based financial system beyond the U.S. dollar’s reach is gaining traction as a way to sidestep sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Krolik

media:content.media:credit: Lincoln Agnew

item.title: Once a Soloist, a Ballerina Finds Her Partner for Life
item.description: Soon after they started dating nearly three years ago, Olivia MacKinnon, a dancer with the New York City Ballet, discovered Bill Keenan’s genuine passion for ballet.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova

item.title: It Started with a Midnight Swim and a Kiss Under the Stars
item.description: Marian Lurio and Jonathan Nguyen met at a mutual friend’s wedding four years ago and felt an immediate attraction.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Goodman

item.title: ‘Sparks Flew’ When Two Crushes Reunited After Three Decades
item.description: Elizabeth Maresca and Nicholas Magarelli liked each other in high school, but they never dated. They reconnected in 2017. Both were divorced. Neither was looking for love.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: Quote of the Day: Iranian Expats in Dubai Feel Caught Between Home and Homeland
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, April 24, 2026.

item.title: Trump Announces Cease-Fire Between Israel and Lebanon
item.description: President Trump said on Thursday that Israel and Lebanon had agreed to a three-week extension to a cease-fire. Hezbollah did not comment on the announcement.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The cease-fire, which was scheduled to end on April 26, would last until May 17 if it takes effect as President Donald Trump described it.

item.title: Tornado Hits Enid, Oklahoma, as Severe Weather Threatens Central U.S.
item.description: The tornado damaged homes and struck an Air Force base in Enid, Okla., the local sheriff said. Some people were injured.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado

item.title: Dan Goldman Will Personally Match Each Donation as He Fights to Keep House Seat
item.description: Daniel Goldman is pledging at least $1 million of his own money, and possibly millions more, in hopes of fighting off a primary challenge from Brad Lander in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Daniel Goldman, who represents parts of Manhattan and Brooklyn, is one of the richest members of Congress.

item.title: Pentagon Fires Stars and Stripes Newspaper’s Ombudsman
item.description: The newspaper’s ombudsman, Jacqueline Smith, said she had been given no reason for her dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Valerie Plesch for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Pentagon has been taking steps to increase its editorial control of Stars and Stripes, which has covered the military continuously since World War II.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 24, 2026
item.description: Andrew Spooner returns to open our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Valery Hache/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Stretch and warm up your brain for this Friday puzzle’s clever misdirects.

item.title: Review: ‘The Rocky Horror Show’ Revival Gives Us What We Want
item.description: Sam Pinkleton’s new revival at Studio 54 gives us the big gay mayhem we want while also maintaining some order via Rachel Dratch’s droll Narrator.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw and Sara Krulwich

item.title: Poetry Challenge Day 5: The Role of Poetry In Our Lives
item.description: Now you can join Matthew McConaughey, Ada Limón, David Sedaris and other luminaries in bringing these lines to life. You’ll never look at the night sky the same way again.
item.dc:creator: A.O. Scott and Aliza Aufrichtig

item.title: Trump Reposts Anti-Immigrant Tirade Calling China and India ‘Hellhole’ Places
item.description: The president touched off a furor with his post sharing a podcast episode in which the host referred to China and India as “hellhole” places.
item.dc:creator: Amy Qin

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump posted the transcript on his Truth Social account on Wednesday night.

item.title: Read the Indictment Charging a U.S. Soldier With Insider Trading
item.description: The soldier, Master Sgt. Gannon Ken Van Dyke, who was stationed at Fort Bragg in North Carolina, made more than $400,000 by betting on different outcomes related to Venezuela after learning of the operation, federal prosecutors and the F.B.I. said.

item.title: Un soldado usó información clasificada para apostar por la captura de Maduro, según EE. UU.
item.description: Los fiscales federales afirman que el sargento Gannon Ken Van Dyke, quien participó en la operación para capturar a Nicolás Maduro, usó la información para hacer apuestas en un mercado de predicciones.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Weiser and Jonah E. Bromwich
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Nicolás Maduro, expresidente de Venezuela, escoltado desde un helicóptero para ser conducido al tribunal federal de Manhattan en enero.

item.title: Trump Says Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Cease-Fire by Three Weeks
item.description: The president made the announcement after he hosted a meeting at the White House of Israeli and Lebanese diplomats.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The cease-fire, which was scheduled to end on April 26, would last until May 17 if it takes effect as President Donald Trump described it.

item.title: Conservative Lobbyist Hired Man to Violently Extort Client, U.S. Says
item.description: Before his arrest last month, Josh Nass, a lawyer, used his connections to lobby the Trump administration, including for a client seeking clemency.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar and Kenneth P. Vogel

media:content.media:credit: Travis Dove for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Josh Nass, a conservative lobbyist, has used his connections to lobby the Trump administration on behalf of his clients seeking clemency.

item.title: Iran War Has Drained U.S. Supplies of Critical, Costly Weapons
item.description: The Pentagon’s rush to rearm its Mideast forces makes it less ready to confront potential adversaries like Russia and China, administration and congressional officials say.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Jonathan Swan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The remains of a university building in Tehran. Two independent groups say the U.S. expense of the war in Iran so far is between $28 billion and $35 billion.

item.title: Trump Officials Investigate N.Y.C. Schools Over Pro-Palestinian Group
item.description: The federal Department of Education said it would begin a civil rights investigation, reviewing teachings by a group known as N.Y.C. Educators for Palestine.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Department of Education, which is presided over by Linda McMahon, the secretary of education, has clashed with New York City’s schools.

item.title: ‘Apex’ Review: Taron Egerton on the Hunt for Charlize Theron
item.description: Taron Egerton descends into full-tilt madness as a killer hunting Charlize Theron in this Netflix thriller set in southeastern Australia.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Yu

item.title: The Small Changes Readers Made for Better Health
item.description: We asked. Over 800 of you answered.
item.dc:creator: Jancee Dunn

item.title: EE. UU. podría revocar la ciudadanía de cientos de personas
item.description: El gobierno de Trump está asignando casos de desnaturalización a fiscales ordinarios, lo que podría provocar una oleada de personas que serían despojadas de la ciudadanía estadounidense.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Londoño and Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:credit: Kent J. Edwards/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Personas recitando un juramento durante una ceremonia de naturalización en el Liberty State Park de Jersey City, Nueva Jersey

item.title: Trump Says He’s Renovating ‘Filthy’ Reflecting Pool on the National Mall
item.description: The pool, which is more than 2,000 feet long, was the site of Martin Luther King Jr.’s “I Have a Dream” speech and other events.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump discussed renovations to the reflecting pool near the Lincoln Memorial during an event on health care on Thursday.

item.title: Soldier Used Classified Information to Bet on Maduro’s Ouster, U.S. Says
item.description: Federal prosecutors say that Sgt. Gannon Ken Van Dyke, who was involved in the operation to oust Nicolás Maduro from power in Venezuela, used the information to place bets on a prediction market.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Weiser and Jonah E. Bromwich
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Nicolás Maduro, former President of Venezuela, being escorted off a helicopter to be taken to the federal courthouse in Manhattan in January.

item.title: Un choque mortal muestra los profundos lazos entre EE. UU. y México en materia de seguridad
item.description: El suceso reabrió el debate sobre la soberanía y la cooperación entre dos naciones que comparten mucho más que una larga frontera.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas
item.category: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives

media:content.media:credit: Procuraduría General de Chihuahua
media:content.media:description: Un presunto laboratorio de procesamiento de metanfetamina en el norteño estado mexicano de Chihuahua, el estado donde dos agentes de la CIA murieron en un accidente automovilístico este mes.

item.title: Intel’s Revenues Soar, Aided by A.I. Boom
item.description: The chip maker reported a 7 percent rise to $13.6 billion in its latest quarter, more than $1 billion more than Wall Street expected.
item.dc:creator: Don Clark

media:content.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Intel’s manufacturing campus in Ocotillo, Ariz., where a federally subsidized expansion is underway.

item.title: Doug Allan, Polar Cameraman for David Attenborough’s Films, Dies at 74
item.description: He was renowned for his skill at capturing candid scenes of penguins, polar bears and other cold-weather creatures — and for his ability to tolerate extreme discomfort.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: Court Revives D.C.’s Ban on High-Capacity Gun Magazines, for Now
item.description: The Trump administration and D.C. officials, normally at odds over the city’s strict gun laws, had both asked the court to reconsider the case.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron

media:content.media:credit: Rich Pedroncelli/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A semiautomatic rifle with a 25-round magazine, left, and a 10-round magazine. Many common models of guns have standard magazines above the 10-round capacity that Washington, D.C., allows.

item.title: How the Iran War Is Morphing Into a Volatile Standoff in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The conflict has morphed into a volatile standoff in the Strait of Hormuz, as the economic costs mount and President Trump faces a political backlash at home.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A mural in Tehran illustrates Iran’s grip on the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Carney Snaps Back at Trump Officials as Canada and U.S. Squabble Over Trade Deal
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney shot back at Trump administration officials, saying Canada is not ‘taking instructions from the United States.’
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen and Ana Swanson

media:content.media:credit: Ian Austen/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada at a housing announcement in suburban Ottawa on Thursday.
